description: Fluke 5440B Direct Voltage Calibrator - a precision DC voltage standard providing calibrated outputs from microvolts to 1100 V, with voltage-boost (to higher current) and current-boost modes when used with a 5205A/5220A amplifier. IEEE-488 (1978) interface. The Calibrator uses Fluke's own legacy device-dependent command set (Table 4-7), NOT SCPI: messages are five-character-max alphabetic headers (e.g. SOUT, OPER, STBY, DIVY) optionally followed by a numeric/integer argument. Several device-dependent messages may be combined into one statement separated by the separator character (default comma ','); a statement executes only when terminated and must be under 127 characters. Get* headers cause the Calibrator to queue a response that is sent when it is next addressed to talk. Incoming lower-case is folded to upper-case and the parity bit is ignored.

identity.description: The 5440B predates IEEE 488.2 and has no '*IDN?' or other command returning a fixed model-identification string. The nearest feature is GVRS (Get Version), which returns only the Calibrator software version number in the format 'dd.dd' - not a model name or manufacturer - so it cannot be used as a reliable instrument-identity query.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Aborts the statement being executed and returns the Calibrator to the power-on / RESET-key state (Standby, internal sense and guard, divider off, etc.). Equivalent to pressing the front-panel RESET key.

commands.name: set_separator
commands.description: Defines the ASCII character recognised as the separator between device-dependent messages within a statement. SSEP0 selects comma ',' (default); SSEP1 selects semicolon ';'.

commands.name: get_separator
commands.description: Queries the currently selected separator character; the Calibrator returns it on the IEEE-488 interface when next addressed to talk.

commands.name: set_terminator
commands.description: Defines the terminator appended to messages the Calibrator sends. 0 = END (EOI) with last data byte, 1 = CR with END on last LF (default), 2 = END with last LF, 3 = CR LF, 4 = LF.

parameters.name: terminator
parameters.description: 0=END on last byte, 1=CR/END-on-LF (default), 2=END on last LF, 3=CR LF, 4=LF

commands.name: get_terminator
commands.description: Queries the currently selected output terminator; the Calibrator returns the corresponding integer code on the IEEE-488 interface.

commands.name: boost_current_mode
commands.description: Enables Current Boost mode (same as BOOST then CURR BOOST soft key). Disables Voltage Boost mode and ordinary Voltage mode. In this mode SOUT arguments are in amps.

commands.name: boost_voltage_mode
commands.description: Enables Voltage Boost mode (same as BOOST then VOLT BOOST soft key). Disables Current Boost mode and ordinary Voltage mode.

commands.name: boost_off
commands.description: Disables boost operation and enables ordinary Voltage mode.

commands.name: set_output
commands.description: Sets the Calibrator output to the value of the numeric argument and auto-ranges to suit. The unit is volts in Voltage and Voltage Boost modes, and amps in Current Boost mode. SOUT does not set the error-mode reference; to set the reference send SOUT followed by SREF. Mantissa under eight significant digits; absolute value between 1E-12 and 1E8.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Output level. Volts in Voltage/Voltage-Boost mode (0 to +-1100 V); amps in Current Boost mode. Accepts decimal or scientific notation.
parameters.range: +-1100 V (voltage modes); current-mode value per amplifier; |value| in 1E-12..1E8

commands.name: get_output
commands.description: Queries the present output value; the Calibrator sends it as a mantissa on the IEEE-488 interface when next addressed to talk.

commands.name: increment_output
commands.description: Adds the numeric argument to the present output value (relative step). Unit follows the active mode (volts or amps).

parameters.name: delta
parameters.description: Amount added to the present output. Volts in voltage modes, amps in current-boost mode.

commands.name: set_reference
commands.description: Stores the present output value as the error-mode reference. Used with the error-mode (% of reference) calculations; typically sent after SOUT to fix the nominal value being characterised.

commands.name: get_reference
commands.description: Sets the Calibrator output back to the stored error-mode reference value.

commands.name: operate
commands.description: Places the Calibrator in Operate (same as the OPR/STANDBY key lit): the programmed output is connected to the OUTPUT / SENSE terminals.

commands.name: standby
commands.description: Places the Calibrator in Standby (same as the OPR/STANDBY key un-lit): the output is removed from the terminals. Standby is the power-up/RESET default for safety.

commands.name: external_sense
commands.description: Selects external (4-wire remote) sensing (same as SENSE key lit). The Calibrator regulates voltage at the remote SENSE terminals.

commands.name: internal_sense
commands.description: Selects internal (2-wire) sensing (same as SENSE key un-lit). The Calibrator regulates voltage at its own OUTPUT terminals.

commands.name: external_guard
commands.description: Selects external guard (same as GUARD key lit): the internal guard is opened so the user provides the guard connection.

commands.name: internal_guard
commands.description: Selects internal guard (same as GUARD key un-lit): the internal guard is shorted to output low.

commands.name: divider_enable
commands.description: Turns on the internal output divider (used for low-voltage / high-accuracy 2 V and 0.2 V ranges).

commands.name: divider_disable
commands.description: Turns off the internal output divider.

commands.name: set_voltage_limit
commands.description: Sets a voltage limit (same as LIMIT then VOLT LIMITS soft key) to the numeric value. In Voltage/Voltage-Boost mode the polarity of the argument selects whether the positive or negative limit is set; send one positive and one negative entry to bound both. Not recognised in Current Boost mode.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Voltage limit; sign selects the positive or negative limit.

commands.name: get_voltage_limit
commands.description: Queries the voltage limit(s); the Calibrator sends the value(s) on the IEEE-488 interface. In Voltage Boost mode the response is two mantissas (positive then negative limit); in plain Voltage mode it is a single mantissa.

commands.name: set_current_limit
commands.description: Sets a current limit (same as LIMIT then CURR LIMTS soft key) to the numeric value. In Voltage mode it is a current-trip threshold (absolute value of the argument). In Current Boost mode the argument polarity selects the positive or negative limit. Not recognised in Voltage Boost mode.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Current limit / trip value; in current-boost mode sign selects positive or negative limit.

commands.name: get_current_limit
commands.description: Queries the current limit(s); the Calibrator sends the value(s) on the IEEE-488 interface. In Current Boost mode the response is two mantissas (positive then negative limit); in Voltage mode it is a single mantissa.

commands.name: set_tolerance_floor
commands.description: Sets the error-mode tolerance floor (same as ERROR MODE then ENTER TOL soft key) to the fractional value of the numeric argument. Combined with SPRF to form the pass/fail tolerance band. Value is a dimensionless fraction.

parameters.name: floor
parameters.description: Tolerance floor as a dimensionless fraction.

commands.name: get_tolerance_floor
commands.description: Queries the error-mode tolerance floor; the Calibrator sends it as a mantissa on the IEEE-488 interface.

commands.name: set_percent_of_reference
commands.description: Sets the error-mode percentage-of-error specification to the value of the numeric argument (a dimensionless fraction). Combined with SFLR to form the tolerance band used by the % error pass/fail (GEPF) test.

parameters.name: fraction
parameters.description: Percent-of-reference error spec, expressed as a dimensionless fraction (e.g. .0001 = 0.01%).

commands.name: get_percent_of_reference
commands.description: Queries the error-mode percent-of-reference specification; the Calibrator sends it as a mantissa on the IEEE-488 interface.

commands.name: get_percent_error
commands.description: Queries the calculated percent error of the present output from the stored reference; the Calibrator sends the result as a fraction (mantissa) on the IEEE-488 interface.

commands.name: get_setup_status
commands.description: Queries the Setup Status. The Calibrator sends the sum of the active setup codes (Table 4-5): 1=Voltage mode, 2=Current Boost mode, 4=Voltage Boost mode, 8=Divider on, 16=Internal sense (not external), 32=Operate (not standby), 64=Internal guard shorted to low, 128=Rear output active. Returned as an integer.

commands.name: get_error
commands.description: Queries the remote error status. The Calibrator resets all remote error flags and sends the error code as one to three decimal digits on the IEEE-488 interface (see Section 2 for error code meanings).

commands.name: get_version
commands.description: Queries the Calibrator software version number; sent in the format 'dd.dd' on the IEEE-488 interface. This is a firmware version, not a model identity string.

commands.name: get_doing_state
commands.description: Queries what long-term operation the Calibrator is performing (any operation longer than ~5 s, e.g. internal calibration, self test). Returns an integer state code per Table 4-6 (0 = Idle). Receiving GONG also clears the Doing State Change SRQ bit.

commands.name: get_percent_error_pass_fail
commands.description: Queries the error-mode pass/fail result; the Calibrator sends 1 for pass or 0 for fail on the IEEE-488 interface, based on the present output versus the SFLR/SPRF tolerance band.

commands.name: calibration_internal
commands.description: Starts the Internal Calibration procedure (self-cal of the A/D and ranges). Progress can be monitored with GONG; this is a long-term operation.

commands.name: calibration_external
commands.description: Starts the External Calibration procedure, in which the operator enters the value of an external voltage standard (via CSTD) for each range.

commands.name: calibration_standard
commands.description: During external calibration, supplies the value of the external calibration standard voltage as the numeric argument.

parameters.name: standard
parameters.description: Measured value of the external voltage standard for the current cal range.

commands.name: calibrator_nulled
commands.description: During external calibration, tells the Calibrator that it has been nulled against the external voltage standard for the present range (advances the cal sequence).

commands.name: calibration_skip_range
commands.description: During external calibration, skips the present calibration range without changing its stored constant.

commands.name: get_calibration_constant
commands.description: Queries a stored calibration constant identified by the integer argument; the Calibrator sends the constant as a mantissa on the IEEE-488 interface. Refer to the Service Manual for the constant index meanings.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Integer index identifying which calibration constant to read.

commands.name: test_analog
commands.description: Executes the analog self-test. On failure the Calibrator sends an error code on the IEEE-488 interface; on pass there is no response. Completion can be detected when GONG returns idle.

commands.name: test_digital
commands.description: Executes the digital self-test. On failure the Calibrator sends an error code; on pass there is no response. Completion can be detected when GONG returns idle.

commands.name: test_high_voltage
commands.description: Executes the high-voltage self-test. WARNING: hazardous voltage may appear at OUTPUT HI / SENSE HI during this test. On failure an error code is sent; on pass there is no response. Completion can be detected when GONG returns idle.

commands.name: get_baud_rate
commands.description: Queries the RS-232-C baud-rate code; the Calibrator sends the integer on the IEEE-488 interface.

commands.name: monitor_on
commands.description: Turns the RS-232-C monitor on: internal-calibration and analog-test data are sent out on the RS-232-C interface.

commands.name: monitor_off
commands.description: Turns the RS-232-C monitor off.

commands.name: set_service_request
commands.description: Sets the SRQ Mask to enable interrupts for the conditions selected by the integer (sum of: 0=never, 4=doing a long-term operation, 8=requested message ready to send, 16=output settled, 32=error condition). Default mask value is 0.

parameters.name: mask
parameters.description: SRQ mask: sum of 4 (long-term op), 8 (message ready), 16 (output settled), 32 (error). 0 disables all.

commands.name: get_service_request_mask
commands.description: Queries the SRQ Mask; the Calibrator sends its value as an integer on the IEEE-488 interface.

commands.name: get_serial_poll_byte
commands.description: Queries the Serial Poll Status Byte; the Calibrator sends its decimal equivalent on the IEEE-488 interface. Bit weights: Request for Service, Error Condition, Output Settled, Message Ready To Send, Doing State Change (see Figure 4-6).
